name: spine twist
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engage your abs and slowly twist your torso to the right, bringing your right elbow towards your left knee.
Pause for a moment at the end of the twist, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat the twist to the left side, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you. Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart. Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards. Engage your abs and slowly twist your torso to the right, bringing your right elbow towards your left knee. Pause for a moment at the end of the twist, then slowly return to the starting position. Repeat the twist to the left side, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
